title: Pagination View Models, Immutable State, Map Transitions, and Stale Results · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A public science-learning team incrementally builds immutable stale-safe Pokemon page state from original fixed PokéAPI payloads while retaining prior TypeScript, HTTP, Git, repository-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of immutable stale-safe Pokemon page state while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Convert validated transport pages into readonly resource, cursor, result identity, count, and source metadata.
Derive candidate navigation and commit only a validated successful page while preserving prior state on failure.
Assign operation identity or generation so late responses cannot overwrite current state after newer commands.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 8327 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to commit immutable validated pages while excluding failed and out-of-order stale results; each Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, and handle needs explicit ownership; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for convert validated transport pages into readonly resource, cursor, result identity, count, and source metadata.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Script rapid next, back, abort, failure, short page, repeated view, and out-of-order responses and assert every state.

hints: Name the Pokedex Ts Pagination Sequence Tests precondition or invariant that the workshop case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A workshop TypeScript team handles case 1660 during an inclusive museum learning kiosk. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to commit immutable validated pages while excluding failed and out-of-order stale results; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, and packed artifact; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Script rapid next, back, abort, failure, short page, repeated view, and out-of-order responses and assert every state.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Testing one awaited command at a time covers async pagination.
